top_strategic.title: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
top_strategic.evidence: The camera sees these items but we detect no brands. ZERO detections: accessories, apparel, footwear, wristband. Near-zero: sunglasses(1). Each gap = a brand that could be paying for visibility data.
top_strategic.action: Add shoe/apparel/sunglasses brand detection examples to Gemini prompt — immediate revenue unlock

top_strategic.title: Facility Owner Idea Lab — SaaS for Court Operators
top_strategic.evidence: Package the internal Idea Lab as a white-labeled SaaS dashboard ($2K–$5K/month per facility). Facility owners see: 'your top 3 players are undermonetized (coaching opportunity)', 'Nike players win 12% more than average', 'weekday 6pm slots have highest viral content potential.' The facility clicks 'approve' to pitch sponsors or 'dismiss' to ignore. We train the AI on their feedback. Suddenly every facility becomes a mini research lab feeding us ground truth.

top_strategic.title: Sponsorship whitespace detected in 2498 clips (95.2%) — sell to brands
top_strategic.evidence: We detect unbranded equipment/space in 2498 clips. This is the gap between what's visible and what's branded — sellable data.
top_strategic.action: Package sponsorship whitespace data as 'brand opportunity report' for equipment companies

top_strategic.title: Endorsement intelligence: 131 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
top_strategic.evidence: Top pairs: PickleBill-JOOLA(574x), Chintan-JOOLA(293x), Ironvarr-JOOLA(175x), Tmf-JOOLA(145x), Santinofuntila-JOOLA(136x)
top_strategic.action: Build 'brand ambassador identification' product for equipment companies

top_strategic.title: The Player Passport — Portable Identity & Achievement Layer
top_strategic.evidence: Players mint a blockchain-backed passport of their game stats, badges, and highlight clips that travel ACROSS facilities and platforms (not locked in Courtana). We charge players $4.99/month for a premium passport with predicted rankings, endorsement-ready highlights, and tournament eligibility proofs. Brands sponsor badge categories ('fastest serve,' 'most consistent volley'). Facilities compete to host players with high passport scores.

top_data.title: Viral shot: 'overhead' clips average 5.29 viral vs 'none' at 1
top_data.evidence: Shot viral averages: overhead=5.29, speed_up=4.45, reset=4, volley=3.58, dink=3.03, drive=2.92, serve=1.6, unknown=1.2, none=1
top_data.action: Prioritize highlight reels featuring high-viral shot types

top_data.title: Prompt fix: 5 badges consistently missed — add positive examples
top_data.evidence: Production awards these but DaaS misses them: 'rally master' (6 missed), 'new look' (5 missed), 'steady eddie' (4 missed), 'paddle tap celebration' (3 missed), 'fresh fit' (3 missed). These are real badges that our prompt doesn't detect.
top_data.action:
Add positive examples to Gemini prompt:
'When analyzing badges, make sure to check for these commonly missed badges:
  - rally master: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - new look: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - steady eddie: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - paddle tap celebration: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - fresh fit: Look for [describe visual pattern]

top_data.title: DaaS badge prediction: F1=0.98 (precision=0.99, recall=0.97)
top_data.evidence: Across 364 verified clips: 1376 correct predictions, 16 false positives, 41 missed badges. Overall F1=0.980.
top_data.action: Target recall improvement: add more badge examples to Gemini prompt

top_data.title: Worst predicted badges: third shot drop artist (F1=0.933), hustler (F1=0.857)
top_data.evidence: Lowest F1 badges: third shot drop artist (F1=0.933), hustler (F1=0.857). These need prompt improvement.
top_data.action: Add specific examples for these badge types in Gemini prompt

top_data.title: Net/venue branding inflates 6 brands — true share-of-court differs from raw counts
top_data.evidence: Brands with significant venue-only detections (not product use): JOOLA (353/4380 are venue), Nicol (6/14 are venue), PUSHIT (5/9 are venue), adidas (4/47 are venue), 7 (3/21 are venue). Raw counts overstate market share. Product-use only: JOOLA=4027, Nike=436, PICKLEBALL=120, HEAD=83, Foz_Bullet=62.
top_data.action: Report TRUE share-of-court (product-use only) to brand partners, not raw detection counts

all_findings.title: Viral formula: quality (7.42 vs 4.89) + watchability (6.71 vs 2.85)
all_findings.evidence: Top 100 viral clips: quality=7.42, watchability=6.71. Bottom 100: quality=4.89, watchability=2.85. Both metrics track strongly with viral potential.
all_findings.action: Use (quality + watchability) / 2 as quick viral prediction score

all_findings.title: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
all_findings.evidence: The camera sees these items but we detect no brands. ZERO detections: accessories, apparel, footwear, wristband. Near-zero: sunglasses(1). Each gap = a brand that could be paying for visibility data.
all_findings.action: Add shoe/apparel/sunglasses brand detection examples to Gemini prompt — immediate revenue unlock

all_findings.title: Prompt fix: JOOLA appears in 265/364 verified clips — add disambiguation
all_findings.evidence: JOOLA dominates 73% of clips. Prompt should distinguish: paddle logo (real JOOLA use), net logo (JOOLA sponsors nets), and venue branding (JOOLA sponsors the facility).
all_findings.action:
Add to brand detection prompt:
'For JOOLA specifically, classify detection source:
  - paddle: JOOLA logo on paddle face = confirmed product use
  - net: JOOLA logo on net = venue sponsorship, NOT product use
  - venue: JOOLA on walls/banners = facility sponsorship
Only count paddle detections as brand_category: paddle'

all_findings.title: Viral arc: 'top_play' is 150.0x more likely in top clips (15.0% vs 0.0%)
all_findings.evidence: Story arc 'top_play' appears in 15.0% of top-viral clips but only 0.0% of bottom-viral clips.
all_findings.action: Prioritize 'top_play' clips in automated highlight reels

The facility clicks 'approve' to pitch sponsors or 'dismiss' to ignore. We train the AI on their feedback. Suddenly every facility becomes a mini research lab feeding us ground truth.
all_findings.action: Evaluate: platform_play play

all_findings.title: Brand-skill split: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM users excel at kitchen (7.5), PSA users at power (6.29)
all_findings.evidence: Among brands with 10+ clips: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM has highest avg kitchen mastery (7.5), while PSA has highest power rating (6.29).
all_findings.action: Build brand-skill correlation report for brand partnerships

all_findings.title: Top content creators: PickleBill leads with 354 clips
all_findings.evidence:
PickleBill: 354 clips, viral=3.2, Steady Eddie
Chintan: 186 clips, viral=3.4, Dink Specialist
Ironvarr: 81 clips, viral=3.4, Kitchen Master
Santinofuntila: 67 clips, viral=4.0, Dink Specialist
Tmf: 59 clips, viral=3.5, Kitchen Master
all_findings.action: Create player spotlight feature for top contributors

all_findings.title: Signature moves = viral gold: 2595 clips with signatures avg 3.0 viral vs 3.9
all_findings.evidence: 2595 clips have identified signature moves, averaging 3.0 viral vs 3.9 for clips without.
all_findings.action: Auto-tag signature move clips as premium content

all_findings.title: Skill standout: Ajr averages 8.5 in consistency across 6 clips
all_findings.evidence: Ajr skill profile: consistency=8.5, kitchen=8.2, touch=8.0, composure=7.5, court_iq=7.0, court_coverage=6.2, athleticism=5.2, power=3.0, creativity=3.0
all_findings.action: Feature Ajr in coaching product showcase

all_findings.title: Viral clips have more brand detections: 2.1 vs 1.4 per clip
all_findings.evidence: Top-viral clips average 2.1 brand detections, bottom-viral average 1.4.
all_findings.action: Investigate if brand presence correlates with production quality or camera angle

all_findings.title: Longitudinal tracking possible: 53 players with 5+ clips
all_findings.evidence: 53 players have enough data for improvement tracking. Top: PickleBill(354), Chintan(186), Ironvarr(81), Santinofuntila(67), Tmf(59)
all_findings.action: Build player improvement trajectory product — track skill changes over time

all_findings.title: Rally length matters: long rallies are more viral (long=3.2 vs short=2.2)
all_findings.evidence: Avg rally: 15.8 shots. Long rallies (15+): 1346 clips, avg viral 3.2. Short (4-): 55 clips, avg viral 2.2.
all_findings.action: Use rally length as a quality signal in highlight curation

all_findings.title: Best predicted badges: lob recovery duo (F1=1.0), dink master (F1=1.0), speed up specialist (F1=1.0)
all_findings.evidence: Top performing badges by F1 score: lob recovery duo (F1=1.0), dink master (F1=1.0), speed up specialist (F1=1.0)
all_findings.action: Use these badges as confidence anchors for the prediction system

all_findings.title: Arc-specific prompt issue: 'strategic' clips cause 94% of false positives
all_findings.evidence: The 'strategic' arc type produces disproportionate badge false positives (15/16 = 94%). The prompt may be misinterpreting this arc type's visual patterns.
all_findings.action: Add arc-specific badge guidance: 'For strategic clips, badge X is common but Y is not'

connections.strategic_finding: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
connections.data_finding: Prompt fix: 5 badges consistently missed — add positive examples
connections.combined_insight: Combine 'Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment cate' with 'Prompt fix: 5 badges consistently missed' for a targeted product.

product_concept.name: BadgeBoost
product_concept.first_screen: Dashboard showing 5 missed equipment badge categories (e.g., 'Paddle Brands', 'Shoe Brands') with side-by-side comparison: current detection rate vs. potential with positive examples added. A 'Train Now' button lets users upload 10-20 reference images per category to retrain the detection model.
product_concept.why_powerful: Turns undetected sponsorship opportunities into immediately actionable training data by closing the gap between what the model sees and what's actually on court.

connections.strategic_finding: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
connections.data_finding: Net/venue branding inflates 6 brands — true share-of-court differs from raw counts
connections.combined_insight: Combine 'Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment cate' with 'Net/venue branding inflates 6 brands — t' for a targeted product.

product_concept.name: CourtShare Analyzer
product_concept.first_screen: Side-by-side brand exposure comparison showing 'Raw Detection Count' vs. 'Adjusted Court Share %' for 6 brands, with a toggle revealing which detections were net/venue artifacts vs. genuine player/equipment placements. Sponsors see their true competitive position in a sortable leaderboard.
product_concept.why_powerful: Reveals that sponsorship valuations based on raw brand mentions are systematically wrong, enabling sponsorship teams to negotiate fairly and identify genuinely unsponsored equipment categories.

connections.strategic_finding: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
connections.data_finding: Prompt fix: JOOLA appears in 265/364 verified clips — add disambiguation
connections.combined_insight: Combine 'Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment cate' with 'Prompt fix: JOOLA appears in 265/364 ver' for a targeted product.

product_concept.name: JOOLAssistant
product_concept.first_screen: Search-and-tag interface where users see JOOLA product mentions across 265 verified clips, with a dropdown to disambiguate: 'JOOLA Paddle', 'JOOLA Apparel', or 'JOOLA Venue/Other'. Each tagged instance trains a secondary classifier; a progress bar shows disambiguation coverage per category.
product_concept.why_powerful: Converts a single brand's overwhelming presence into structured, category-specific sponsorship intelligence that unblocks detection for competing brands in underrepresented equipment categories.
